posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You just spent 6 hours on a project.

You sent the invoice.

And the moment you hit send вЂ” you wondered if you should have charged more.

That feeling isn't imposter syndrome. It's a pricing problem.

Most freelancers charge based on time, nerves, or what they think clients will accept. None of those are a strategy. All of them leave money on the table вЂ” every single project, every single month.

The fix isn't confidence. It's a framework.

Value-based pricing links your fee to what the work is actually worth to the client вЂ” not to how many hours it took you. A copywriter who writes an email sequence that generates $40,000 in launch revenue isn't worth $50/hour. They're worth a percentage of that outcome.

When you understand that, your pricing changes permanently.

We built a free guide that gives you the exact system: how to calculate client value, how to choose the right pricing model, how to write proposals that justify premium rates, and how to handle every objection without backing down.

It's called the P.R.I.C.E.в„ў Framework. And it's free.

рџ‘‰ Get it here: https://publixion.com/guides/difficulty-pricing-services

#Freelancing #PricingStrategy #ValueBasedPricing #FreelancerLife #Publixion
posts.facebook:
You worked hard. You delivered great work.

And you still don't know if you charged enough.

Every freelancer knows this feeling. It lives in the gap between what you billed and what you could have billed вЂ” and it compounds every single month.

The problem isn't your skill. It's that nobody taught you how to price.

Hourly rates punish you for getting faster. Cost-based pricing ignores what the client actually gains. And guessing вЂ” which most freelancers do вЂ” means you're always leaving money somewhere.

Value-based pricing changes the entire equation. You stop selling time and start selling outcomes. Your price becomes a reflection of what the client gains вЂ” not what it costs you to deliver.

We put the full system into a free guide: the P.R.I.C.E.в„ў Framework вЂ” 5 steps to price any service confidently, write proposals that get accepted, and handle objections without discounting.

posts.linkedin:
Most freelancers price wrong because they ask the wrong question.

They ask: "How much should I charge for this?"

The right question is: "What is this worth to the client?"

Here's the P.R.I.C.E.в„ў Framework from our free pricing guide вЂ” 5 steps that answer that question for every project:

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
P вЂ” Position Your Value
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Before you quote anything, identify the measurable outcome your service delivers. Not what you do вЂ” what changes for the client. Revenue earned, time saved, risk reduced. A web designer doesn't build websites. They build sales infrastructure. Price that.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
R вЂ” Research the Market
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Map competitor pricing вЂ” not to copy it, but to understand the floor. The goal is to position above average, not race to the bottom. Survey past clients on willingness to pay. One honest client conversation is worth more than hours of online research.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
I вЂ” Identify Your Structure
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Hourly rates cap your income and penalise expertise. Project-based pricing gives clarity. Value-based pricing вЂ” where your fee is linked to client ROI вЂ” unlocks premium rates. The formula: Price = Client Outcome Value Г— 10%.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
C вЂ” Craft Your Proposal
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Every proposal follows one structure: Problem в†’ Outcome в†’ Investment. Your price appears last вЂ” after the value is already established. The client should feel that NOT hiring you is the riskier choice.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
E вЂ” Execute and Negotiate
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Send the proposal confidently. When they push back вЂ” and they will вЂ” redirect from cost to value. Scripts for every objection are included in the free guide.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

This is the system that takes freelancers from "I hope they accept this" to "I can defend this number in any conversation."

Free. Full breakdown.

рџ‘‰ https://publixion.com/guides/difficulty-pricing-services

#FreelancePricing #ValueBasedPricing #FreelancerTips #Consulting #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
Let me show you exactly how the P.R.I.C.E.в„ў Framework works in practice.

Meet Tariq.

Tariq is a freelance UX designer. 4 years of experience. Strong portfolio. He charges $65/hour and typically works 35 hours per project. That's $2,275 per client вЂ” a number he arrived at by looking at what other designers charge on LinkedIn.

He reads the Value-Based Pricing guide on a Sunday night.

Here's what changes by his next client conversation:

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
STEP 1 вЂ” He runs the P.R.I.C.E.в„ў Value Calculation
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
His next prospect is a SaaS founder whose onboarding flow has a 38% drop-off rate. Tariq estimates that fixing the flow would reduce churn by 15% вЂ” worth approximately $60,000/year to the business.

The guide's formula: Price = Client Outcome Value Г— 10%.

That puts his fee at $6,000 вЂ” not $2,275.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
STEP 2 вЂ” He restructures his proposal
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Instead of: "UX audit and redesign of onboarding flow вЂ” $2,275."

He writes: "Your current onboarding drop-off is costing you an estimated $60,000/year in churn. This redesign project targets a 15% retention improvement. The investment is $6,000."

Same work. Different frame. The price makes sense now вЂ” it's 10% of the problem.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
STEP 3 вЂ” The client pushes back
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
"That's more than we budgeted."

Tariq uses the guide's objection script: "I understand. Let me walk you through the outcome this investment is designed to deliver. If we hit a 15% churn improvement, you recover this fee in under 5 weeks. Does that context change how you're looking at it?"

The client accepts the proposal at $6,000.

Tariq just delivered the same work he always delivers вЂ” for 2.6Г— his previous rate.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

The system is in the free guide. The next project is yours to reprice.

рџ‘‰ https://publixion.com/guides/difficulty-pricing-services

#FreelancePricing #UXDesign #ValueBasedPricing #Freelancing #Publixion
posts.facebook:
Here's what applying the P.R.I.C.E.в„ў Framework actually looks like.

Imagine you're a freelance copywriter. You currently charge $300 per email sequence. You've been doing this for 2 years. The rate feels safe вЂ” clients don't push back much.

You read the Value-Based Pricing guide.

Here's what changes:

рџ“Њ STEP 1 вЂ” You calculate client outcome value
Your next client has a product launch coming up. They have 8,000 email subscribers. Industry average conversion on a good email sequence is 2вЂ“4%. At a $97 product price, a 2% conversion = $15,520 in launch revenue.

The guide's formula: your fee = 10% of client value = $1,552.

You've been charging $300 for the same work.

рџ“Њ STEP 2 вЂ” You rewrite your proposal
Old version: "7-email welcome sequence вЂ” $300."

New version: "A 7-email launch sequence designed to convert your 8,000-subscriber list at 2вЂ“4%, generating an estimated $15,000вЂ“$31,000 in launch revenue. Investment: $1,500."

Same deliverable. Completely different framing.

рџ“Њ STEP 3 вЂ” Client accepts
No negotiation. No pushback. Because $1,500 is obviously reasonable when the expected return is $15,000.

You just 5Г— your rate without changing your service.

Full system in the free guide.

рџ“– https://publixion.com/guides/difficulty-pricing-services

#Freelancing #Copywriting #PricingStrategy #FreelanceTips #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
"My clients won't pay more."

I hear this constantly. Let me address it вЂ” and every other objection вЂ” directly.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
вќЊ "My clients won't pay higher rates."
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Your current clients hired you at your current rate вЂ” because that's what you presented. A different frame produces a different response. When the proposal leads with client ROI instead of your time investment, price becomes a business decision, not a budget objection. The guide includes the exact proposal language that makes this shift.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
вќЊ "I don't have the experience to justify higher prices."
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Value-based pricing isn't about your years of experience вЂ” it's about your client's outcome. A junior developer who automates a task saving a company 20 hours/week has delivered more value than a senior developer who builds something nobody uses. Price the outcome, not the resume.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
вќЊ "I'll lose clients if I raise my rates."
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
You will lose some clients. That is the point. The goal is not to keep every client вЂ” it's to keep fewer, better-paying clients. Every freelancer who has made this transition reports working fewer hours for more income. The guide walks you through how to transition existing clients without burning relationships.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
вќЊ "What if they say it's too expensive?"
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
The guide includes a full objection script table вЂ” the exact words to say to "too expensive," "I need cheaper," "I can get this elsewhere," and "I need to think about it." You'll never be caught off guard again.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
вќЊ "This guide is free вЂ” how good can it actually be?"
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Fair question. It includes: the full P.R.I.C.E.в„ў framework, 3 case studies with real numbers, a value calculation formula, a proposal template, objection scripts, worksheets, and a 7-day action plan. Judge it for yourself.

вќ“ "My clients won't pay more."
They won't pay more for your time. They will pay more for a guaranteed outcome. The guide shows you how to reframe every proposal around client ROI вЂ” which changes how price lands completely.

вќ“ "I don't have enough experience to charge premium rates."
